name: Legacy Code Explainer
description: Explains what legacy or unfamiliar code does in plain English — functions, side effects, and gotchas included.
author: BobSkills Community
skill_md:
---
name: legacy-code-explainer
description: Explains what legacy or unfamiliar code does in plain English
author: BobSkills Community
version: 1.0.0
---

Analyze the provided code and produce a clear explanation:

- What this code does (1-2 sentence summary)
- Function-by-function or block-by-block breakdown
- Any hidden side effects or global state mutations
- Known risks or code smells
- Suggested renames for clarity if names are cryptic

<Steps>
  <Step>Read through the full code without assumptions</Step>
  <Step>Identify the entry point and execution flow</Step>
  <Step>Document each logical block in plain English</Step>
  <Step>Flag anything dangerous or surprising</Step>
  <Step>Return structured explanation</Step>
</Steps>

name: Unit Test Generator
description: Generates comprehensive unit tests for any function or module — happy path, edge cases, and error conditions.
author: BobSkills Community
skill_md:
---
name: unit-test-generator
description: Generates comprehensive unit tests for any function or module
author: BobSkills Community
version: 1.2.0
---

Generate unit tests for the provided code:

- Detect the language and testing framework in use (Jest, Vitest, Mocha, pytest)
- Cover: happy path, boundary values, null/undefined inputs, error throwing
- Use descriptive test names that read like documentation
- Mock external dependencies where appropriate
- Output a ready-to-run test file

<Steps>
  <Step>Identify language, framework, and function signatures</Step>
  <Step>List all meaningful test cases before writing code</Step>
  <Step>Write happy path tests first</Step>
  <Step>Add edge case and error condition tests</Step>
  <Step>Output complete test file with imports</Step>
</Steps>

name: API Documentation Writer
description: Produces clean, structured API documentation from route handlers or controller code — with params, responses, and examples.
author: BobSkills Community
skill_md:
---
name: api-documentation-writer
description: Produces clean, structured API documentation from route handlers or controller code
author: BobSkills Community
version: 1.0.0
---

Generate API documentation for the provided route or controller:

- Endpoint: method + path
- Description: what this route does
- Request: headers, params, query, body with types
- Response: status codes, response shape, example JSON
- Error cases: what fails and why
- Output in Markdown format suitable for README or Notion

<Steps>
  <Step>Identify all routes and HTTP methods</Step>
  <Step>Extract request parameters and body schema</Step>
  <Step>Document all response shapes and status codes</Step>
  <Step>Write example request and response blocks</Step>
  <Step>Output clean Markdown documentation</Step>
</Steps>

name: PR Impact Analyzer
description: Analyzes a code diff or PR and summarizes the impact — what changed, what could break, and what reviewers should focus on.
author: BobSkills Community
example_input:
- removed: if (user.role === 'admin') return next();
+ added: if (user.role === 'admin' || user.beta === true) return next();
skill_md:
---
name: pr-impact-analyzer
description: Analyzes a code diff or PR and summarizes impact
author: BobSkills Community
version: 1.0.0
---

Analyze the provided diff or PR code:

- Summarize what changed in plain English (2-3 sentences)
- Identify files or modules with highest blast radius
- Flag any deleted or modified public interfaces
- Highlight anything requiring a migration or config change
- Suggest what tests should be run or added
- Rate risk level: Low / Medium / High

<Steps>
  <Step>Parse the diff and categorize changes by type</Step>
  <Step>Identify public-facing changes vs internal refactors</Step>
  <Step>Assess downstream impact on callers and consumers</Step>
  <Step>Flag missing tests or documentation updates</Step>
  <Step>Output structured impact report with risk rating</Step>
</Steps>

name: Security Vulnerability Scanner
description: Scans code for common security vulnerabilities — injection, auth flaws, exposed secrets, and OWASP Top 10 patterns.
author: BobSkills Community
skill_md:
---
name: security-vulnerability-scanner
description: Scans code for common security vulnerabilities — injection, auth flaws, exposed secrets, OWASP Top 10
author: BobSkills Community
version: 1.3.0
---

Scan the provided code for security issues:

- SQL / NoSQL / command injection vectors
- Hardcoded secrets, tokens, or credentials
- Insecure direct object references
- Missing input validation or sanitization
- Auth and authz bypass risks
- Output: severity (Critical / High / Medium / Low) + line reference + fix suggestion

<Steps>
  <Step>Scan for injection patterns and unsanitized inputs</Step>
  <Step>Search for hardcoded credentials or env leaks</Step>
  <Step>Check authentication and authorization logic</Step>
  <Step>Review third-party imports for known risk patterns</Step>
  <Step>Output prioritized vulnerability report with fixes</Step>
</Steps>

name: Feature Implementation Planner
description: Takes a feature description and produces a step-by-step implementation plan with file changes, risks, and time estimates.
author: BobSkills Community
example_input: Feature: Add JWT authentication to an Express API. Users should be able to register, login, and access protected routes.
skill_md:
---
name: feature-implementation-planner
description: Takes a feature description and produces a step-by-step implementation plan
author: BobSkills Community
version: 1.0.0
---

Produce an implementation plan for the described feature:

- Break feature into discrete, sequenced tasks
- For each task: what files change, what the change is, estimated effort
- Identify blockers and dependencies between tasks
- Flag integration risks or areas needing extra care
- Suggest a testing strategy
- Output as a numbered checklist ready to execute

<Steps>
  <Step>Parse the feature description and identify scope boundaries</Step>
  <Step>List all affected modules, routes, components, or schemas</Step>
  <Step>Sequence tasks in dependency order</Step>
  <Step>Estimate effort per task as S, M, or L</Step>
  <Step>Output executable checklist with risk flags</Step>
</Steps>

name: Bug Root Cause Tracer
description: Given a bug description and relevant code, traces the root cause, explains the failure chain, and suggests a fix.
author: BobSkills Community
skill_md:
---
name: bug-root-cause-tracer
description: Traces a bug to its root cause, explains the failure chain, and suggests a fix
author: BobSkills Community
version: 1.1.0
---

Trace the root cause of the described bug:

- Restate what the bug actually is, not just symptoms
- Walk through the failure chain: what triggers what
- Identify the exact line or condition causing the failure
- Explain why the current code behaves this way
- Propose a minimal fix
- Note any related code that may have the same issue

<Steps>
  <Step>Understand the expected vs actual behavior</Step>
  <Step>Trace execution path from trigger to failure</Step>
  <Step>Identify the root cause line or condition</Step>
  <Step>Propose fix with minimal side effects</Step>
  <Step>Check for similar patterns elsewhere in code</Step>
</Steps>

name: Onboarding Guide Builder
description: Reads a codebase or module and generates a new-developer onboarding guide — architecture, setup steps, and where to start.
author: BobSkills Community
example_input: Project: Express REST API with MongoDB. Stack: Node.js, Express, Mongoose, ESM. Has routes for users, products, orders.
skill_md:
---
name: onboarding-guide-builder
description: Generates a new-developer onboarding guide from a codebase or module description
author: BobSkills Community
version: 1.0.0
---

Generate an onboarding guide for the provided codebase or module:

- Project purpose in 2 sentences
- Tech stack summary
- Local setup steps (numbered, copy-pasteable)
- Folder structure walkthrough: what lives where and why
- Key files every dev must understand first
- Common tasks: how to add a route, component, or test
- Where NOT to touch and why

<Steps>
  <Step>Identify the tech stack and project type</Step>
  <Step>Extract setup requirements from config files</Step>
  <Step>Map the folder structure to responsibilities</Step>
  <Step>Identify the 5 most important files for a new dev</Step>
  <Step>Output complete onboarding guide in Markdown</Step>
</Steps>

name: Code Review Assistant
description: Reviews any code submission like a senior engineer — flags bugs, bad patterns, naming issues, and suggests concrete improvements.
author: BobSkills Community
skill_md:
---
name: code-review-assistant
description: Reviews code like a senior engineer — bugs, bad patterns, naming issues, concrete improvements
author: BobSkills Community
version: 1.1.0
---

Review the provided code as a senior engineer would:

- Identify bugs or logic errors
- Flag violations of clean code principles (naming, single responsibility, DRY)
- Spot performance issues or unnecessary complexity
- Note missing error handling or edge case gaps
- Suggest concrete rewrites for the worst offenders
- Rate overall code quality: Poor / Needs Work / Good / Excellent

<Steps>
  <Step>Read the full code and understand its intent</Step>
  <Step>List all bugs and logic errors found</Step>
  <Step>Flag style, naming, and structural issues</Step>
  <Step>Identify missing error handling or edge cases</Step>
  <Step>Write improved version of the worst sections</Step>
  <Step>Output structured review with overall quality rating</Step>
</Steps>

name: Database Schema Designer
description: Takes a plain English description of your data and produces a clean, normalized database schema with field types, relationships, and indexes.
author: BobSkills Community
example_input: I need a database for a food delivery app. Users can place orders containing multiple items from restaurants. Each restaurant has a menu. Riders deliver orders.
skill_md:
---
name: database-schema-designer
description: Converts plain English data descriptions into clean, normalized database schemas
author: BobSkills Community
version: 1.0.0
---

Design a database schema for the described system:

- Identify all entities and their attributes
- Define relationships: one-to-one, one-to-many, many-to-many
- Choose appropriate field types for each column
- Apply normalization (avoid redundancy)
- Suggest indexes for performance-critical fields
- Output as SQL CREATE TABLE statements or Mongoose schemas depending on DB choice
- Include an entity relationship summary in plain English

<Steps>
  <Step>Extract all entities from the description</Step>
  <Step>Define attributes and data types for each entity</Step>
  <Step>Map relationships and foreign keys</Step>
  <Step>Normalize to at least 3NF where applicable</Step>
  <Step>Suggest indexes on frequently queried fields</Step>
  <Step>Output schema in the requested format with ER summary</Step>
</Steps>

name: JavaScript to TypeScript Converter
description: A comprehensive skill for converting JavaScript code to fully typed TypeScript with proper type annotations, interfaces, and type safety.
author: BobSkills Community
skill_md:
---
name: js-to-typescript
description: Converts JavaScript code to fully typed TypeScript with proper type annotations, interfaces, and type safety
author: BobSkills Community
version: 1.0.0
---

Convert the provided JavaScript code to TypeScript:

- Rename files from .js to .ts (or .tsx for JSX/React)
- Add type annotations to all function parameters and return types
- Create interfaces for object shapes and complex data structures
- Use generics for reusable, type-safe functions and classes
- Replace any with proper types, unknown, or generics
- Add access modifiers (public, private, protected) to class members
- Install @types packages for third-party dependencies
- Ensure strict mode compliance with no implicit any

<Steps>
  <Step>Analyze the JavaScript code structure and identify functions, classes, and data patterns</Step>
  <Step>Rename file extensions and update import statements</Step>
  <Step>Add type annotations to function parameters and return types</Step>
  <Step>Define interfaces and type aliases for data structures</Step>
  <Step>Convert classes with proper typing and access modifiers</Step>
  <Step>Implement generics where applicable for type safety</Step>
  <Step>Install type definitions for external dependencies</Step>
  <Step>Run TypeScript compiler and fix all type errors</Step>
  <Step>Verify strict mode passes without errors</Step>
</Steps>
